---
name: Desktop macOS crash fix and content format bridge
description: RCTThirdPartyComponentsProvider nil crash fix + BlockNote/TipTap content format conversion for desktop editor
type: project
originSessionId: 2a82f59e-25b4-424a-93ef-3afd37541c07
---

Desktop macOS app had a native crash (NSInvalidArgumentException: nil object at index 3) when clicking a note.

**Root cause:** `RCTThirdPartyComponentsProvider.mm` (auto-generated by React Native codegen) uses NSDictionary literal with `NSClassFromString()` values. iOS-only classes like `RNSFullWindowOverlay` return nil on macOS, crashing the dictionary init.

**Fix:** Patched ALL copies of codegen template + generator across node_modules (react-native, react-native-macos, and nested copies) via Podfile post_install + Fastfile post-cocoapods safety net. Template uses NSMutableDictionary; generator emits nil-safe `{ Class c = NSClassFromString(...); if (c) dict[key] = c; }`.

**Why:** The patches must cover react-native-macos (not just react-native) because that's the fork used for macOS builds. Also, codegen runs during pod install, so Podfile post_install patches templates BEFORE codegen uses them. The Fastfile also patches the generated file after cocoapods as a belt-and-suspenders approach.

**Content format:** Web uses BlockNote (JSON array), desktop uses TenTap/TipTap. `contentToTiptap()` and `tiptapToBlocknote()` from `@drafto/shared` handle conversion. Desktop must convert on both load AND save to stay compatible with web.

**How to apply:** These patches live in Podfile post_install (templates/generators) and Fastfile (generated file). They survive `pnpm install` and `pod install` but must be re-verified if react-native or react-native-macos versions change.

**Data corruption incident (2026-04-13):** Before the format converter was wired up, the desktop auto-save wrote TipTap-format content to the DB, corrupting one note ("Svenska" → "2026/04/09"). Fixed by extracting the original BlockNote JSON from the text nodes and re-saving via `fix-note.js`.
